ead.archdesc:
======= RMM00347Kaercher and Packer family papers347Compiled by N. DeanDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
December 20022B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
relig slave lifesThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:39:08 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Kaercher and Packer family papers
Kaercher family.
Packer family.
347
2.5 cubic feet.
4 volumes.
2 mapcase folders.
1789-1938. Correspondence, commissions, reports, licenses, financial accounts, sermons, deeds, agreements, veterinary formulas, diaries, essays, poetry, and other items. Correspondence and other papers relating the life of George Kaercher, who was a divinity student at Burr Seminary in Manchester, Vermont and Western Reserve College in Ohio, and later a minister in Ohio. Also includes papers of the Packer family. Collection material in English 2097236
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
N. Dean
Date completed:
February 1998
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, December 2002
Date modified:
RMC Staff, August 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
George Kaercher was a divinity student at Burr Seminary, Manchester, Vermont, and Western Reserve, Ohio and a minister in Ohio.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters to Kaercher commenting on education, economic and social conditions, the costs of travel, revivals among students and laymen, Taylorism (New Haven theology), Millerism (Millerite movement), the need of ministers among German immigrants of Ohio and Pennsylvania, the American Tract Society, religion on the frontier, epidemics, the Reverend Albert Barnes, Dr. Tyng, and other divines; commissions and reports to the American Home Missionary Society, licenses to preach, accounts as to expenses and salaries; correspondence of Rev. Kaercher during the Civil War with the Woman's Central Association of Relief, sermons, and a few Anti-Masonic and Anti-Slavery items. Also, Packer family papers, including deeds, agreements, accounts, receipts, personal and business correspondence of the Packer, Mason, and Kaercher families; veterinary formulas of Johnathan Packer; includes diaries, essays, poetry, and miscellanea. Families are centered in Preston, Chenango County, New York.
Cite As:
Kaercher and Packer family papers, #347. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Diaries. Sermons. Chenango County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs. Chenango County (N.Y.) -- Social conditions. Chenango County (N.Y.) -- Economic conditions. Clergy. Veterinary drugs. Revivals. Immigrants. New Haven theology. Millerite movement. Antislavery movements. Freemasonry. Families -- New York (State) -- Preston. Theological seminaries. Kaercher, George. Barnes, Albert. Packer, Johnathan. Mason family. Burr Seminary Western Reserve College American Tract Society American Home Missionary Society Woman\'s Central Association of Relief
2097236 <<<<<<< HEAD B, Woodruff and Packer. Deeds, letters, account, receipts, 1789-1912 and no date.1789-19121A 4-5, Sermons of Rev. George Kaercher.2A 1-3, Kaercher papers.1 religious poem written in German -1816311 letter - May 8, 1838 - John W. Elliot to George Kaercher - from Manual Labor Inst., Marion, Ala. - lack of educational advantages in South - opportunity for "enterprising merchant, mechanic, or professional man"May 8, 1838312 letters - Feb. 12 and Dec. 13, 1834 - from Williams College - Oliver M. Sears to Kaercher - college life, religious excitement, aid for those studying for the ministry at WilliamsFeb. 12 1834Dec. 13, 1834311 letter - 1839 - Hudson, Ohio - unsigned to Kaercher - costs of going from Albany to Ohio, living costs at Western Reserve, etc. - idea of Kaercher laboring among own people in West - muddy roads in Ohio - Prof. St. John and collection of mineral specimens1839311 letter - Oct. 1839 - to Kaercher - received $15 aid from American Education Society while at Burr SeminaryOct. 1839311 pledge - Nov. 1839 , Manchester, Vt. - by students to pray, on separation, for each member of group for one yearNov. 1839311 letter - Dec. 16, 1839 - Western Reserve - Jacob Patch to Kaercher - division of church in Sharon - religious schism - division among congregations of churches - urges Kaercher to bring whole class with him to Western Reserve - college literary societiesDec. 16, 1839311 letter - Feb. 25, 1840 - Watertown - Guernsey to Kaercher - religious revival - sermons of M. GrovsnorFeb. 25, 184031Expenses at Burr Seminary -Mar. 25, 184031Receipt for work -July 4, 1841311 letter - July 27, 1840 - Amenia - Hiram Weed to Kaercher - on aid from home people to Kaercher - hard times - furnacesJuly 27, 1840311 letter - July 9, 1840 - Upton, Mass. - M. Grosvenor conducting good revival in regionJuly 9, 1840311 letter - Oct. 10, 1840 - Burr Seminary - uses shorthand - epidemic (?) - many deathsOct. 10, 1840311 letter - Mar. 13, 1841 - ManchesterMar. 13, 1841311 letter - Mar. 28, 1841 - Aaron Read to Kaercher urging him to sing in gallery in church, not to leave it, that singing before long will be from front of pulpitMar. 28, 1841311 letter - July 31, 1841 - S.N. Grant to Kaercher - Storatton - Kaercher has been helping to conduct revivals - state of Burr Seminary - 4th July celebration at Townsend (3 or 400,000 present)July 31, 1841311 letter - May 6, 1842 - Manchester - Grant - condition of Burr Seminary - no religious revival, revival at Williams College (only 20 students there impenitent)May 6, 1842311 letter - Apr. 25, 1843 - Sharon - Hiram Weed to Kaercher - Presbyterians and revival - (Methodists influenced by Millerism) - Christ to come this month - not many to believe this part "yet the multitude that came to hear from curiosity were willing to attend from night to night that they might hear the truth from Mr. Reed and others"Apr. 25, 1843311 letter - June 9, 1843 - Savanah - Christine Carlen to Kaercher at Western Reserve - husbandJune 9, 1843311 letter - Oct. 1, 1843 - Sharon - Hiram Weed - Post Office creates excitement - revival died downOct. 1, 1843311 letter - Oct. 24, 1843 - Detroit - Lucious C. Rouse - had proposed to American Tract Society that Kaercher work during vacation in Stack Co. - ATS refused, wanting permanent works - Rouse urges Kaercher to preach to Germans in Ohio or Penn. and consult with German Reformed or Presbyterians Church - number of Germans in Springfield and Canton - urges Kaercher to spend vacation in exploration trip among these Germans and in giving away tractsOct. 24, 1843311 citizenship papers - George Kaercher - May 21, 1844 , Akron, OhioMay 21, 1844321 licence to preach - July 24, 1844 - Hudson - G. Kaercher - by Presbytery of PortageJuly 24, 1844321 letter - Oct. 21, 1844 - Yale - Guernsey to G. KaercherOct. 21, 1844321 commission from Home Missionary Society - Dec. 21, 1844 - G. Kaercher - six months at Northampton, Summit Co., Ohio - to receive $25 and rent from congregationDec. 21, 1844321 letter - Mar. 14, 1845 - Guernsey to Kaercher - Kaercher's brother Frederick arrested for counterfeiting moneyMar. 14, 1845321 letter - Aug. 4, 1845 - Guernsey to Kaercher - description of Albert Barnes, Stephen Higginson, Dr. TyingAug. 4, 1845321 diploma - Aug. 14, 1845 - G. Kaercher - Western Reserve CollegeAug. 14, 1845321 commission from Home Missionary Society - Sept. 1, 1845 - 1 year at Peninsula - $100Sept. 1, 1845321 letter - Oct. 22, 1845 - Yale - Guernsey to Kaercher - New School Presbyterian Church at Newburgh, N.Y.Oct. 22, 1845321 letter - Nov. 20, 1845 - Lima, Mich. - E.P. Marvin - on advantages of minister's lifeNov. 20, 1845321 license - Nov. 24, 1845 - State of Ohio - to Kaercher to perform marriagesNov. 24, 1845321 letter - Jan. 20, 1846 - Yale - Blakeslee to Kaercher - likes Wm. Taylor as lecturer but condemns TaylorismJan. 20, 1846321 letter - Jan. 26, 1846 - Wolcottville, Conn. - theory of annihilation of the wicked - on salariesJan. 26, 1846321 letter - July 7, 1846 - New Haven - Blakeslee - Westerner by birth, nothing would keep him in East - influence of Reserve in Ohio, on Lakes and Mississippi Valley - "For the people of the Reserve have the intelligence and enterprise of North East combined with the energy and go-a-headativeness of the West"July 7, 1846321 letter - Aug. 24, 1846 - Knoxville, Knox Co., Ill. - R.C. Bristol to Kaercher - describes state of churchesAug. 24, 1846321 commission - Sept. 1, 1846 - G. Kaercher - 1 year at Peninsula, Ohio, for $100Sept. 1, 1846321 quarterly report to American Home Missionary Society - Dec. 11, 1846 - Peninsula - G. Kaercher - Methodists held protracted meeting - quite a lot of sicknessDec. 11, 1846321 letter - Mar. 8, 1847 - H.K. Smith to Kaercher - formation of a Teacher's Institute for schoolteachersMar. 8, 1847331 letter - Dec. 28, 1846 - Northampton - R.C. Bristol - preached in bar using bar for pulpitDec. 28, 1846331 quarterly report to AHM - asks for leave to visit sick mother -June 9, 1847331 letter - July 26, 1847 - R.C. Bristol - urges Kaercher to come to Illinois - great need for ministersJuly 26, 1847331 commission - Nov. 1, 1849 - G. Kaercher - Peninsula - 1 year - $75Nov. 1, 1849331 letter - June 21, 1848 - Henry Reid to Kaercher - presents plan for him to come to Waterville and preach half the time and spend half under the direction of American Tract SocietyJune 21, 184833Additional papers333 letters -1850Hiram Weed to G. Kaercher - Sharon34Joel Case to G. Kaercher - Victoria, Tex.34Eunice Ford to G. Kaercher343 letters -1851S. Wright to G. Kaercher - New Berlin34Charles Cunty to G. Kaercher - Peninsula34Aaron Reed to G. Kaercher - Sharon341 commission - 1851 - to G. Kaercher - Preston - 1 yr. 501851341 petition - 1851 - to American Home Missionary Society for payment of $50 to Kaercher - congregation to pay $2001851343 letters -1852Catherine Kaercher to G. Kaercher - father34Catherine Kaercher to G. Kaercher - father34Eunice Ford to G. Kaercher342 letters -1854George and Cornelia Kaercher to Lucy Packer34Lucy Packer to George and Cornelia Kaercher345 letters - 1854 - George Kaercher to his wife, Cornelia1854341 letter - 1855 - Catherine Kaercher to G. Kaercher185534Additional papers341 letter - 1856 - G. Kaercher to nephew - Preston1856354 letters -1857Wm. Potter to G. Kaercher - Claridon35G.W. Roberts to G. Kaercher - Green, N.Y.35H.W. Gilbert to G. Kaercher - Green, N.Y.35J. Patch to G. Kaercher - Orland, Ind.354 letters -1858J. Patch to G. Kaercher - Orland, Ind.35Ellen to Cornelia Kaercher - Cleveland35Edward Baymal to G. Kaercher - concerning death of Kaercher's sister, Catherine Baymal, and son35C.A. Hernandes to G. Kaercher - Savannah - concerning death of Kaercher's sister352 letters -1859G. Kaercher to his wife - Tabery35G. Kaercher to nephew - Preston352 letters - 1862 - Woman's Central Ass. of Relief to G. Kaercher1862358 letters to G. Kaercher from Elisabeth Wilbur and children355 letters -1864Mary Fairbanks to Cornelia Kaercher35S. Wait to G. Kaercher - Four Mill Grove35J.K. Mason to G. Kaercher352 letters - G. Kaercher to his mother - Sharon354 letters -1866Sharon - George Wilbur - to G. Kaercher36Tomah - F. Ebert to G. Kaercher36Tomah - Daniel Ebert to G. Kaercher36Pottsville - George Kaercher to George Kaercher364 letters -1868Catherine Field to G. Kaercher - Trempealeau36Charles A. Throop to G. Kaercher - Trempealeau36James A. Throop to G. Kaercher - Trempealeau36Helen Noyes to Cornelia Kaercher362 letters -1869Daniel Ebert to G. Kaercher36Aaron Reed to G. Kaercher362 letters -1874Daniel Ebert to G. Kaercher36D. Vrooman to G. Kaercher362 letters - 1878 - G. Kaercher to family1878363 letters - Mary Fairbanks to Cornelia Kaercher - Barnabas Horves Jr. to George Kaercher37Constitution of the Social and Benevolent Society of the Town of Preston37Report of number of bibles in families of church37List of subscribers for benefit of George Kaercher378 miscellaneous pieces37B, Woodruff and Packer. Justice of the Peace records, elections records, etc., Preston, NY, 1817-1890 and no date.1817-18904Mss., diries of Kaercher, Mason, Noyes, and Packer families, 1817-1922 and no date.1817-19224Volumes, assessment book, 1848 ; genealogical information on Beaumont, Mason, Packer, Pratt, Woodruff and other families,18481764-1940.5Scrapbooks, copybooks, 1836-1913 and no date. Also list of voters Town of Preston, Oct. 13, 1863 ; "The German Church Friend for American German Churches," Jan.-Dec. 1850 , 11 issues.1836-1913Oct. 13, 1863Jan.-Dec. 18506Photographs.7Misc. printed material on Centennial Exhibition, Philadelphia, Pa., 1876 , souvenir item.18767Four loose mss. vols.Loose mss. vol.1Loose mss. vol.2Loose mss. vol.3Loose mss. vol.4 ======= B, Woodruff and Packer.
